name: Streamarr
description: Monitor Twitch channels for livestreams & VODs, auto-create/remove channels. Dual detection: Twitch Helix API (fast batch) or streamlink CLI fallback.

settings.label: Monitored Twitch Channels
settings.helper_text:
One Twitch username per line. Format: username or username=BaseNumber (for decimal numbering). Lines starting with # are ignored.

Examples:
nightbot
pokimane=50
montanablack88=60

⚠ Without Twitch API credentials, each channel is checked individually via streamlink. This is slow for more than ~20 channels - consider adding API credentials.

settings.label: Stream Profile (streamlink)
settings.helper_text:
Select a Stream Profile with the 'Streamlink' backend. FFmpeg profiles will NOT work for Twitch streams.

⚠ You must first create a Stream Profile under Settings → Stream Profiles with backend set to 'Streamlink' and quality 'best'. Cookies configured on this profile will also be used for stream detection.

settings.label: Target Playlist (Standard)
settings.helper_text: Standard playlist to associate created channels with. Leave empty to skip.

settings.label: Target Playlist (Custom)
settings.helper_text: Custom playlist to add created channels to. Leave empty to skip.

settings.label: Group Mode
settings.helper_text: Static uses the fixed group name below. Game mode groups live channels by their current Twitch game/category (e.g. 'Just Chatting', 'Fortnite'). Game mode requires Twitch API credentials and falls back to static without them. VOD channels always use the VOD Group name.

settings.label: Live Channel Group
settings.helper_text: Group name for live-stream channels. Used in static mode, and as fallback in game mode when the game is unknown.

settings.label: Stream Quality
settings.helper_text: Preferred stream quality. Streamlink will select the closest available quality.

settings.label: Include VODs
settings.helper_text: Also create channels for recent VODs of monitored channels. Requires Twitch API credentials - VOD discovery is not available via streamlink fallback.

settings.label: VOD Limit per Channel
settings.helper_text: Maximum number of recent VODs to import per monitored Twitch channel.

settings.label: Auto-cleanup Ended Streams
settings.helper_text: Automatically delete live-stream channels when the stream ends. Runs during each monitoring cycle. VOD channels are never auto-cleaned.

settings.label: Starting Channel Number
settings.helper_text: First channel number to assign. Each new stream increments from here.

settings.label: Channel Number Increment
settings.helper_text: How much to increment the channel number for each new stream.

settings.label: Channel Numbering Mode
settings.helper_text: Decimal mode groups streams from the same Twitch channel using the channel's BaseNumber. Sequential mode uses a global counter.

settings.label: Twitch Client ID
settings.helper_text: Twitch API Client ID for fast batch detection. Get one at https://dev.twitch.tv/console/apps - create an application and copy the Client ID. Leave empty to use streamlink fallback.

settings.label: Twitch Client Secret
settings.helper_text: Twitch API Client Secret. Required together with Client ID for API-based detection.

settings.label: Enable Automatic Monitoring
settings.helper_text: Run monitoring automatically on the schedule below. Requires the scheduler to be running (php artisan schedule:run).

settings.label: Monitor Schedule (cron)
settings.helper_text: Cron expression for automatic monitoring. Default: every 10 minutes. Examples: */5 * * * * (every 5 min), 0 * * * * (hourly).

fields.label: Twitch URL(s)
fields.helper_text: One or more Twitch channel or VOD URLs (newline or comma-separated). Examples: https://twitch.tv/pokimane, https://twitch.tv/videos/123456789
